The snippet search wasn't directly on-target, but the Knowles/Cohen secondary-nucleation scaling is well established: the secondary nucleation flux scales as k₂·m(t)·M(t) where M(t) is aggregate mass — and the aggregate-concentration dependence produces the sharp autocatalytic knee. Making the secondary term superlinear in the state (concentration²) is the standard way to sharpen the sigmoidal onset that the shape_loss rewards. This is enough to justify the implementation from first principles; I'll proceed.

I'll implement the breakthrough exactly as specified: quadratic secondary term with a saturable denominator, autonomous (no time term), fixed plateau capacity, 9 constants.
